channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Online shopping grew to $1.2 trillion over the holiday season, Salesforce found
item.description: Online sales hit record highs—$1.2 trillion globally and $282 billion in the U.S.—over the holiday season, but high return rates could hit strong profit margins, according to new data from Salesforce.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: The European political establishment has had enough of Elon Musk
item.description: Elon Musk has never been shy about sharing his opinions to his 211 million followers on X. But for those who follow him—and those who get his posts pushed into their feeds on the app—the past few weeks have been more unusual than most.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: China protests U.S. Treasury’s sanctions for hacking incidents
item.description: China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cybersecurity agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: UFC boss Dana White joining Meta's board of directors
item.description: Ultimate Fighting Championship (UFC) CEO and President Dana White will join Meta’s board of directors, the social media company announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: NASA’s micro-mission Lunar Trailblazer will scout the Moon this year
item.description: NASA’s upcoming Artemis II mission is slated to return astronauts to the Moon no sooner than April 2026. Astronauts were last on the Moon in 1972 during the Apollo 17 mission.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: HP adds trio of Intel-powered EliteBook laptops as AI PC craze reaches fever pitch
item.description: HP’s EliteBook trio combines Intel-powered AI, sleek displays, and cutting-edge features for professionals.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: Bay Area innovator uses AI to tackle California students’ declining math skills
item.description: Peter Relan, chairman of GotIt! Education, calls MathGPT, "the AI that instructors trust and students love.
item.dc:creator: Molly Gibbs, The Mercury News

item.title: Revolutionizing Rice Farming: How Tech Can End the Gamble
item.description: Meet Soj Gamayan, the 22-year-old Filipino behind AgriConnect, an AI-powered platform that predicts and manages crop risks for rice farmers, aiming to reduce losses from pests, disease, and extreme weather.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: Fubo combining with Disney's Hulu + Live TV; lawsuit against Venu Sports settled
item.description: Disney's Hulu + Live TV and sports streaming service Fubo are combining in a deal that will also see the settlement of a lawsuit against the creation of Venu Sports.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: TikTok creators left in limbo while awaiting decision on potential platform ban
item.description: The fate of TikTok is keeping creators and small business owners in anxious limbo as they await a decision from the Supreme Court that could upheld their livelihoods.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Landrum Jr. And Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How tech breakthroughs changed health in 2024
item.description: The health and technology sectors intersected in fresh ways in 2024, marking a transformation largely driven by the increased use of artificial intelligence (AI).
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: How Big Tech became the world’s most powerful ‘religion’ and why we need to become agnostic
item.description: Greg Epstein is the Humanist chaplain at Harvard University and at MIT, where he advises students, faculty, and staff members on ethical and existential concerns from a humanist perspective.
item.dc:creator: Next Big Idea Club, Fast Company

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took police more than a week to publicly identify Debrina Kawam, 57, as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train last month. But on the internet, it took just hours for a false name to begin spreading.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Chinese cyber firm linked to botnet attack sanctioned in US
item.description: The federal government hit Chinese cyber group Integrity Technology Group with new sanctions on Friday, citing alleged computer intrusion incidents against those in the U.S. At the direction of the China, hackers associated with the Integrity Technology Group allegedly targeted multiple U.S.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Appeals court blocks return of US net neutrality rules for ISPs
item.description: The reintroduction of neutrality rules for network providers is looking increasingly unlikely, at least for the next five years, after a US appeals court blocked efforts by the US Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to reinstate them.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Turo rentals emerge as common thread in Las Vegas Cybertruck and New Orleans deadly incidents
item.description: Turo faces scrutiny after tragic incidents involving rented vehicles, raising questions about security measures and regulatory frameworks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ruby completes switch to Prism parser
item.description: Ruby 3.4.0, and Ruby 3.4.1, updates to the longstanding dynamic, open source programming language, have arrived, with Prism now the default parser. Ruby 3.4.0 was released December 25, Christmas Day, along with an update, Ruby 3.4.1, which merely changed the version number of the release. Ruby 3.4.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Top network and data center events 2025
item.description: Ready to travel to gain hands-on experience with new networking and infrastructure tools? Tech conferences – in person and virtual – give attendees a chance to access product demos, network with peers, earn continuing education credits, and catch a celebrity keynote or live entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Health AI: How Apple can boost public health
item.description: Combined with its existing solutions, Apple’s strategic approach to artificial intelligence (AI) deployment could make a radical difference to public health. Here is how it could achieve that.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Fine-tuning Azure OpenAI models in Azure AI Foundry
item.description: Large language models (LLMs) like the OpenAI models used by Azure are general-purpose tools for building many different types of generative AI-powered applications, from chatbots to agent-powered workflows.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Agencies required to share custom software under new law
item.description: The SHARE IT Act, now law, builds on previous efforts to ensure agencies are sharing common software code across government. The post Agencies required to share custom software under new law first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: The best new Windows 11 features of 2024
item.description: Microsoft is a little sneaky. Sure, there’s just one “big” update for Windows 11 each year. But Microsoft’s Windows team is always working on something, and new Windows 11 features are arriving on PCs every month — even outside of those high-profile updates.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
